title: How I Cracked Microsoft SDE2 in 4-6 Months
content:
Recently cracked Microsoft SDE2 while working full-time and thought of sharing my preparation journey because honestly most advice online either feels too generic or too unrealistic.

[https://youtu.be/FglL5a6mMl0]

In the video I talked about:

* 4-6 months preparation timeline
* LeetCode + GFG approach
* how repeated patterns matter more than random problem solving
* mock interviews
* system design prep (Alex Xu + real work experience)
* mistakes I made
* what actually helped in interviews

One thing I realized after enough interviews is that companies ask different questions but similar patterns keep repeating.

Also gave multiple interviews before Microsoft (including Salesforce) and that experience itself helped a lot with communication and handling pressure.

Made this video mainly for people preparing alongside a full-time job because that changes preparation a lot.

Would genuinely love to know what worked for others here as well.

title: Uber vs Microsoft
content:
Hi everyone,

I’m fortunate to have offers from both Uber and Microsoft and would love some unbiased opinions on which one to choose for long-term growth.

Current offers:

**Uber (L4)**

* Total Comp: ~75 LPA
* Base: 51 LPA (including PF)
* Stocks: 15 LPA/year
* Performance Bonus: 7 LPA
* Relocation: 2 LPA

**Microsoft (L62)**

* Total Comp: ~80 LPA
* Base: 42 LPA
* Stocks: ~26 LPA/year (110K USD total stock grant)
* Joining Bonus: 4 LPA (8LPA for 2 year)
* Performance Bonus: ~4 LPA average
* Relocation: 4 LPA

A few things I care about:

* Quality of engineering work
* Learning and growth
* Brand value in the long run
* WLB and sustainability
* Future opportunities globally

Experience level: ~4.9 years backend engineering.

Would especially appreciate insights from people who have worked at either company recently.
